title: INF-1 LoRA stack composer UI (Inference Lab knob)
goal: Implement the LoRA stack composer view in the Inference Lab UI per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES: ordered list of mounted LoRAs with per-LoRA strength slider, mount-from-disk dialog, unmount, drag-to-reorder, save-as-Work-Profile.
expected_diff_shape: New Svelte component app/src/components/inference_lab/LoraStackComposer.svelte + supporting store app/src/lib/stores/lora_stack.ts; integration into the InferenceLab parent view.
unblock_conditions: MT-068 capability IPC + MT-090 LoRA technique surface merged.
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES + AC-INFER-LAB-UI-AB-COMPARE. LoraStackComposer.svelte: shows the current active stack as an ordered list (drag-to-reorder via svelte-dnd-action or HTML5 drag); each row has [LoRA name, strength slider 0..2, unmount button, license tag badge]; bottom of view has [Mount from disk... button] opening a file picker; [Save as Work Profile] button writes settings.exec_policy.lora_stack. Backed by lora_stack.ts store: subscribes to kernel_model_runtime_lora_list updates (polls every 2s or subscribes to FR events when the SSE bridge is available); commits via kernel_model_runtime_lora_mount/_swap/_unmount. Hidden when capabilities.supportsLora==false (per MT-068 IPC; aligns with AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES "toggles for techniques the model's adapter does not support are hidden, not greyed"). A/B compare per AC-INFER-LAB-UI-AB-COMPARE: clicking a strength slider opens an A/B side-panel showing generation with current strength vs proposed strength on the same prompt; operator commits or discards. License tag badge prominent for adult-production licensing discipline (per GLOBAL-PRODUCTION operator concerns). Backend mutation routes through KernelActionCatalogV1 (no debug-only paths). UI tests via Playwright/CDP (per cluster A.2 RW-4 decision): mount mock LoRA, assert list updates; change strength, assert IPC call fires.
proof_commands: cd app && pnpm exec playwright test --grep lora_stack_composer
risk_if_missed: LoRA UI knob missing; INF-1 not operator-controllable through Inference Lab; AC-INFER-LAB-UI-TOGGLES partial.
validator_focus: Hidden-not-greyed for unsupported adapters; A/B compare flow lands in same Work Profile path; license badge present; KernelActionCatalogV1 routing.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Hidden (display:none) when supportsLora=false — NOT just disabled.
License badge rendered for every entry.
All mutations route through KernelActionCatalogV1 (verify via network tab assertion).

implementation_record.summary: MT-091 implemented the LoRA stack composer surface as a React TSX component (contract said Svelte; same Svelte->TSX defect class as MT-098/102/105/124). Backed by a new app/src/lib/ipc/lora.ts IPC binding that wraps kernel_model_runtime_lora_{mount,unmount,swap,list} with camelCase DTOs and client-side strength range validation. Composer renders only when capabilities.supportsLora === true (hidden, not greyed), shows an ordered list of mounted LoRAs with per-row license badge, strength slider 0..2 step 0.05, unmount button, drag-to-reorder via HTML5 native drag events, a mount-from-disk dialog (Tauri plugin-dialog file picker + descriptor metadata form), and a Save-as-Work-Profile button. Every mutation dispatches through the kernel_model_runtime_lora_* Tauri commands (MT-090), which preflight ModelRuntimeState.lora_command_binding through KernelActionCatalogV1 before touching the live runtime. Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator review remains pending.
contract_drift: Owned files listed Svelte components (LoraStackComposer.svelte + lora_stack.ts store + InferenceLab.svelte). App stack is React/TSX; implemented as LoraStackComposer.tsx + lora.ts IPC binding (React hooks subsume the Svelte store role) + InferenceLab.tsx integration. Same defect class as MT-098/102/105/124.
Proof command listed `pnpm run typecheck`; no `typecheck` script exists in app/package.json. Substituted with `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit`.

Proof command listed `pnpm exec playwright test --grep lora_stack_composer`. The codebase pattern for inference_lab component tests is Vitest + @testing-library/react (see SteeringVectorEditor.test.tsx); Playwright is a devDependency but no inference_lab Playwright spec exists. Implemented as Vitest unit tests in LoraStackComposer.test.tsx covering the same minimum_controls and adversarial scenarios.
design_notes: Descriptor cache: loraList IPC returns light entries only ({loraId, strength, mountedAtUtc}). Full descriptors are required for swap-based reorder and Save-as-Work-Profile (settings.execPolicy.loraStack must carry the descriptor). The composer keeps a client-side descriptor cache populated from mount calls. Rows without a cached descriptor (e.g., LoRAs persisted across app sessions) render an explicit 'Descriptor unavailable — re-mount from disk' warning, disable the strength slider, and disable Save-as-Work-Profile / reorder until the operator re-mounts.

LoRA ID generation: backend uses LoraId::new_v7() (UUID v7). Browser crypto.randomUUID() emits UUID v4 which the backend rejects. Hand-rolled a UUID v7 generator inside LoraStackComposer.tsx (~15 lines) because the app has no UUID v7 npm dependency and adding one for this MT is out of scope.

A/B compare slider (AC-INFER-LAB-UI-AB-COMPARE) is deferred to a follow-up MT. The MT contract called for an A/B side-panel showing generation with current strength vs proposed strength on the same prompt, but no generation IPC is exposed yet. The strength slider UI surface lands here; the A/B side-panel + generation IPC wiring remain open.

License badge is always rendered. When the descriptor is not cached client-side, the badge text falls back to 'license unknown' so the operator still gets a visible licensing indicator (matching the operator-licensing-discipline minimum control).
owned_file_changes: ADDED app/src/lib/ipc/lora.ts (170 lines): IPC binding with camelCase DTOs, LORA_STRENGTH_MIN/MAX constants, assertValidStrength helper, loraMount/Unmount/Swap/List wrappers.
ADDED app/src/components/inference_lab/LoraStackComposer.tsx (~520 lines): composer component implementing all MT-091 owned-file behavior.
ADDED app/src/components/inference_lab/LoraStackComposer.test.tsx (~430 lines, 19 tests): adversarial Vitest unit tests.

frontend_typecheck.note: Clean tsc against the entire app workspace (no MT-091-introduced TS errors; unused-import errors from initial test scaffold cleared in round 3).

focused_vitest.note: 19/19 passed: hidden-not-greyed when supportsLora=false; hidden when capabilities=null; loading + empty + backend-error states; license badge always rendered (incl. 'license unknown' fallback); mount-from-disk happy path with v7 LoRA ID; mount rejected when sha256 malformed; mount rejected when license tag empty (operator licensing discipline); mount rejected when strength out of [0,2]; cancel dialog; dismissed file dialog; strength slider triggers loraSwap with settings.execPolicy.loraStack; strength slider disabled when descriptor uncached; Unmount triggers loraUnmount; Unmount backend error surfaced; Save-as-Work-Profile disabled when descriptor missing; Save-as-Work-Profile dispatches swap with settings.execPolicy.loraStack; drag-to-reorder triggers swap with reordered stack; drag-to-reorder blocked when any LoRA lacks cached descriptor.

full_vitest.note: 63/63 across 15 files passed. No regression from LoraStackComposer / InferenceLab integration.

cargo_core_regression.note: 2/2 passed: technique dispatch + fail-closed when supports_lora is absent. Confirms MT-090 backend still healthy after MT-091 frontend integration.

cargo_tauri_regression.note: 5/5 passed: camelCase DTO + settings.execPolicy.loraStack bridge; mount/list/swap/unmount dispatch; supports_lora=false fail-closed; no-live-runtime typed unavailable; invalid strength pre-mutation rejection.

minimum_controls_evidence.hidden_not_greyed_supports_lora_false: Verified by vitest 'renders nothing when the model adapter does not support LoRA' — component returns null; container.firstChild is null; loraList is not called.
minimum_controls_evidence.license_badge_per_entry: Verified by vitest 'renders the mounted stack with license badge per row' — lora-stack-composer.row.{loraId}.license testid present for every row.
minimum_controls_evidence.mutations_via_kernel_action_catalog: Composer mutations all route through kernel_model_runtime_lora_{mount,unmount,swap,list} Tauri commands. Backend (app/src-tauri/src/commands/lora.rs) preflights ModelRuntimeState.lora_command_binding via KernelActionCatalogV1 before any runtime mutation. cargo_tauri_regression covers the fail-closed path. No mutation bypasses this surface in the composer code.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-091; this record is supporting evidence only.
A/B compare slider (AC-INFER-LAB-UI-AB-COMPARE) is not implemented — needs a separate MT that introduces a generation IPC and the A/B side-panel UI; logged as follow-up scope.
Mount-from-disk requires the operator to paste sha256 by hand. No backend IPC exposes 'compute sha256 of file at path'; logged as follow-up for a hashing helper command.
Mount-from-disk generates a fresh LoRA ID client-side instead of looking it up in a LoRA catalog. There is no LoRA catalog IPC yet; logged as follow-up.

Descriptor cache lives in the React component state; persisted LoRAs that survive an app restart will render with 'Descriptor unavailable' until the operator re-mounts. A future MT could add a backend lora_list IPC that returns full descriptors, eliminating this UX.

Drag-to-reorder uses HTML5 native drag events. Vitest exercises the JS event path; real DOM behavior (touch devices, accessibility) is not covered by these unit tests. Playwright coverage is a deferred follow-up.
Umbrella `just gov-check --sync-topology` still red on six known broad HBR/template/manual checks observed in MT-090; not touched by this MT.

out_of_scope_self_evaluation.why: MT-091 owned files were: LoraStackComposer.svelte, lora_stack.ts (Svelte store), InferenceLab.svelte. Real codebase has no Svelte; React/TSX equivalents (LoraStackComposer.tsx + InferenceLab.tsx + a new IPC binding file) were required to make the MT testable in actual runtime. The new app/src/lib/ipc/lora.ts is outside the owned-file list (the contract did not list any IPC binding file).
touched_outside_mt_owned_files: app/src/lib/ipc/lora.ts (ADDED) — new IPC binding wrapping the MT-090 Tauri commands; required for the composer to dispatch mount/unmount/swap/list.
out_of_scope_self_evaluation.risk_reduced: Prevents the composer from being a paper UI (no backend dispatch) and prevents string-typed invoke calls scattered through the component (centralizes camelCase DTO + strength validation in one binding module, matching the pattern of model_runtime.ts / steering.ts / refusal.ts).

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: LoRA composer UI lands hidden-not-greyed, license badge per row, mutations through KernelActionCatalogV1; A/B compare (AC-INFER-LAB-UI-AB-COMPARE) is deferred — partial spec satisfaction is a real product gap, not paperwork.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: LoRA composer UI lands hidden-not-greyed, license badge per row, mutations through KernelActionCatalogV1; A/B compare (AC-INFER-LAB-UI-AB-COMPARE) is deferred — partial spec satisfaction is a real product gap, not paperwork.
